diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Staphylococcus aureus food poisoning, drug intoxication
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The extremely short incubation period after consuming the meal strongly suggested food poisoning caused by Staphylococcus aureus or Bacillus cereus. Rapid test for 8 drugs yielded negative results, but drug intoxication was considered due to the severity of symptoms. Isolation of B cereus from vomitus, intestinal contents, and leftover fried rice, along with detection of cereulide in serum, confirmed the diagnosis of B cereus emetic food poisoning.
